verdict_reasoning: HMGB1 is canonically a nuclear non-histone chromatin-binding protein, but extensive published evidence documents its translocation to the extracellular space — and transiently to the plasma membrane surface — under specific non-baseline conditions. Active secretion occurs via unconventional pathways (acetylation-dependent vesicular release, exosomal export [PMID 34363018]) from activated immune cells, and passive release occurs during cell death including pyroptosis [PMID 40128620, PMID 30314759]. Before extracellular release, HMGB1 must transit the plasma membrane or be present on the outer leaflet transiently; multiple studies confirm extracellular HMGB1 engages surface receptors (RAGE, TLR4) as a DAMP/alarmin [PMID 35217834, PMID 32265930, PMID 40308590]. This outer-leaflet exposure is strictly cell-state-induced (inflammation, sepsis, cell death, activation) — not constitutive. No TM domain or GPI anchor; baseline localization is nuclear. Thus `contextual` / `cell_state_induced` is appropriate.
